title: Argparse Interface, Help, Options, and Usage Errors · Contract Workshop
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds testable user-facing command interface from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of testable user-facing command interface while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks.
Reject invalid ranges, conflicting flags, unsupported choices, and missing values at the parser boundary with usage evidence.
Write task-oriented usage, descriptions, metavariables, defaults, units, examples, exit behavior, and accessibility notes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Argument Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 1710 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to define and validate a reproducible task-oriented argparse interface with useful help; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks.

# Case 7878: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_python314_argparse_vm87rc(arguments: int = 6, invalid_arguments: int = 3, invalid_rejected: int = 3, help_has_units: bool = True, abbreviations_disabled: bool = True, color_controlled: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_vm87rc = "vm87rc-cr78mv-7878"
    assert evidence_variant_vm87rc
    evidence_axes_vm87rc = (
        "corpusvm87rc",
        "policycr78mv",
        "revision7878",
        "commandvm87rc7878",
        "reportcr78mv7878",
        "test7878vm87rc",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_vm87rc)) == 6
    if arguments < 1 or invalid_arguments < 0:
        return False, arguments, "argument-shape"
    if invalid_rejected != invalid_arguments:
        return False, invalid_rejected, "argument-validation"
    if not help_has_units:
        return False, arguments, "help-contract"
    if not abbreviations_disabled or not color_controlled:
        return False, arguments, "parser-reproducibility"
    assert invalid_rejected == invalid_arguments
    return True, arguments, "testable-cli"
